source_or_gap: Hashed local BeastBrain project snapshot, primary DKL/Portia lineage, and unresolved cross-project/external-literature support for a durable semantic-memory chapter.
chapter_action_policy: Update existing chapters with bounded implementation-reference and negative-case mechanisms after exact mapping. Keep a proposed Durable Semantic Memory and Knowledge Lattices chapter in backlog until all old projects and independent literature are mined.
merge_or_split_policy: Merge planning, routing, SSD resource, benchmark, security, federation, tribunal, and stewardship findings into existing owners. Re-evaluate a new chapter for durable typed semantic memory, ontology, graph/vector retrieval, navigation, lifecycle, and certified snapshots after the full provenance pass.
boundary_decision_rationale: BeastBrain establishes primary provenance for a coherent substrate that is not fully owned by the Virtual Context ABI, claim ledgers, or procedural memory. The candidate boundary is real but should not enter the manifest until remaining projects and independent comparators are reviewed.
external_literature_need: learned graph-navigation and retrieval-routing primary sources
long-term agent-memory and memory-consolidation primary sources
test-time learning and neural-memory primary sources
SSD-paged inference/training and long-context primary sources
secure aggregation and capability-security primary sources
proof_or_test_backlog: record file-and-symbol provenance from BeastBrain DKL/Portia into CCA and MoECOT
classify all 31 crate families by integration and evidence state
rerun a bounded DKL and Portia baseline only if an offline dependency cache is available
compare simulated SSD reports to one real matched small-model baseline before any performance claim
audit retained build errors against the hashed current snapshot
evidence_transition_precondition: exact source surfaces mapped to each book claim
cross-project primary provenance and deduplication completed
independent external sources ingested for any new chapter
numeric project results reproduced or explicitly retained as source-reported context
accepted Appendix C transition recorded before any support promotion
required_before_drafting: mine BugBrain, Corben's Trainer, Corben's Best Model Possible, and any newly extracted projects
keep raw private project material outside the public repository
separate whitepaper design, interface presence, implemented effect, test artifact, and reproduced result
preserve build failures, failed training, ontology drift, simulation labels, and readiness contradictions
promotion_blockers: root Git history has no commit/tree identity
full current workspace compile and tests were not completed
project results are internal and unreproduced
many named mechanisms are partial, simulated, stubbed, or bypassed
cross-project and independent-literature deduplication remain incomplete
source-publication permission is not recorded for the raw tree
residuals: historical compiler failures need current-source classification
TreeLLM datasets/checkpoints/replay environments have not been located
external citations in project whitepapers require separate source ingestion
next_action: Mine BugBrain next, then Trainer and Best Model, before the cross-project provenance and new-chapter decision pass.
non_claims: does not claim BeastBrain capability, safety, security, efficiency, production readiness, AGI, or ASI
does not reproduce project training, benchmark, SSD, memory, routing, hardware, federation, or tribunal results
does not treat a tree digest as a Git commit or signed release
does not add the proposed chapter
does not promote an ASI Stack support state
